_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to evaluate how the features of still and moving images and the use of sound create effects and shape representations.

_source.w: We are learning to evaluate how the features of still and moving images and the use of sound create effects and shape representations.
y: In our media-saturated world, evaluating visual and audio representations is a critical life skill.
Understanding how representations are constructed helps us resist manipulation and think independently.
This evaluative skill applies to everything from news media to advertising to art and entertainment.
eg: We evaluate how camera angles, lighting and composition in a photograph or film construct a particular representation.
We analyse how editing choices in a video — cuts, transitions, pacing — shape the viewer's interpretation.
We discuss how sound design — music, ambient sound, silence — creates emotional effects and shapes meaning.
We evaluate how visual representations in news media can be constructed to serve particular perspectives.
We compare visual and audio treatments of the same subject and evaluate the different effects created.
We create a multimodal text and critically evaluate the representational effects of our own visual and audio choices.
